summary: Today's sweep leans toward AI / privacy, Democracy / press freedom, Education / pedagogy. The strongest items in this run are Judge thwarts Trump administration’s attempt to access Arizona voter rolls, Trump news at a glance: president’s justice department sets sights on James Comey (again), and Live updates from Elon Musk and Sam Altman’s court battle over the future of OpenAI.

mainItems.title: Judge thwarts Trump administration’s attempt to access Arizona voter rolls
mainItems.summary: Justice department’s lawsuit dismissed in latest setback for government’s effort to amass information before midterms Sign up for the Breaking News US email to get newsletter alerts in your inbox An attempt by the Trump administration to…
mainItems.whyItMatters: Touches trust, surveillance, and the private use of AI systems.

mainItems.title: Trump news at a glance: president’s justice department sets sights on James Comey (again)
mainItems.summary: New indictment marks latest instance in which Trump’s justice department has used its power to target president’s political enemies – key US politics stories from 28 April 2026 at a glance The justice department filed new criminal charges…
mainItems.whyItMatters: Relevant to state power, civil liberties, and anti-authoritarian concerns.

mainItems.title: Live updates from Elon Musk and Sam Altman’s court battle over the future of OpenAI
mainItems.summary: Sam Altman and Elon Musk are facing off in a high-stakes trial that could alter the future of OpenAI and its most well-known product, ChatGPT.
mainItems.whyItMatters: Touches trust, surveillance, and the private use of AI systems.

mainItems.title: Linda McMahon punches back at senators questioning Education Department cuts
mainItems.summary: In her first appearance on Capitol Hill this year, lawmakers questioned Education Secretary Linda McMahon about students' civil rights and cuts to federal education spending.
mainItems.whyItMatters: Directly relevant to teaching, classroom practice, and educational judgment.

mainItems.title: As school choice expands in Iowa, one district is in a crisis from losing students
mainItems.summary: With school choice programs ascendant not just in Iowa but across the U.S., Cedar Rapids offers a preview of who wins and who loses when education meets the free market.
mainItems.whyItMatters: Relevant to state power, civil liberties, and anti-authoritarian concerns.

mainItems.title: I tried the first sub-two-hour marathon shoes. Could they help get my running back on track?
mainItems.summary: Two world records were broken in the Adidas super shoes last weekend and the public can soon get their hands on a limited release.
mainItems.whyItMatters: Worth watching for authoritarian drift, selective enforcement, and democratic norms.

mainItems.title: Elon Musk tells the jury that all he wants to do is save humanity
mainItems.summary: On the stand, Elon Musk is positioning himself as a savior. In the high-profile trial between him and his fellow OpenAI co-founder, now CEO, Sam Altman, Musk opened by going through his background.
mainItems.whyItMatters: Relevant to the shape of current AI systems and where the tooling is moving.

mainItems.title: Solve a murder mystery by flying people around on your giant crane in the Disco Elysium and Chinese folktale inspired The Crane Rider's Tale
mainItems.summary: Nothing will put me on to a game quicker than having a concept so laser-focused on the thing it's trying to do, and, seemingly, doing it well.
mainItems.whyItMatters: Worth your time if you care about design, criticism, and not just hype.

mainItems.title: Elon Musk testifies against OpenAI, seeking Sam Altman's ouster
mainItems.summary: Musk's lawyers say OpenAI leaders "stole a charity" and Musk warns about the potential dangers of AI: "We don't want to have a Terminator outcome."
mainItems.whyItMatters: Interesting where engineering, science, and tech infrastructure collide.

mainItems.title: Hawaii Teacher’s Mission: Launching Students Into Space Careers
mainItems.summary: Hawaii Teacher’s Mission: Launching Students Into Space Careers
mainItems.whyItMatters: Directly relevant to teaching, student agency, and school policy.

mainItems.title: Scientists Investigated a Frequency Linked to ‘Paranormal’ Encounters. The Results Were Unsettling.
mainItems.summary: Humans can’t hear low-frequency “infrasound,” but a new study demonstrates that it raises our stress levels and triggers an “unsettling” feeling that could be linked to people’s experiences in haunted locations.
mainItems.whyItMatters: Relevant to your current interest graph.

mainItems.title: So far, Florida has failed to end vaccine mandates. Now there's a last-ditch effort
mainItems.summary: Florida Republicans have pushed hard to drop some vaccine requirements for children. But after many months, their efforts stalled out. Could this week's special legislative session get the job done?
mainItems.whyItMatters: Relevant to your current interest graph.

mainItems.title: Houston, We Have A Problem: Cameron Johnston’s First Mage on the Moon
mainItems.summary: Sasha Bonkowsky reviews the science fantasy novel from Cameron Johnston The post Houston, We Have A Problem: Cameron Johnston’s First Mage on the Moon appeared first on Reactor .
mainItems.whyItMatters: On-theme for your cultural interests, especially when the concept is strong.

trailers.title: The Best of Marty And Doc's Friendship | Back to the Future Trilogy
trailers.summary: Marty McFly (Michael J. Fox) and Doc Brown (Christopher Lloyd) share the ultimate bond as they navigate the dangers of time travel across decades.
trailers.whyItMatters: Potentially worth attention if the trailer looks more interesting than generic studio sludge.

trailers.title: Monarch: Legacy of Monsters — Vinestranglers on Skull Island | Apple TV
trailers.summary: Skull Island is a hostile place where even a nondescript vine is not as it seems. The Vinestrangler scene from "Ends of the Earth." Monarch: Legacy of Monsters Season 2 is now streaming on Apple TV https://apple.
trailers.whyItMatters: Relevant to your current interest graph.

trailers.title: Monarch: Legacy of Monsters — Godzilla Chases Titan X to Skull Island | Apple TV
trailers.summary: He doesn't want to kill her — he just needs her back where she's meant to be. The opening scene of "Ends of the Earth." Monarch: Legacy of Monsters Season 2 is now streaming on Apple TV https://apple.
trailers.whyItMatters: Relevant to your current interest graph.

new.title: Scavengers Reign
new.why: Animated speculative sci-fi with strong worldbuilding and a weirder imagination than most prestige SF.

new.title: Sugar
new.why: Noir detective structure with a genre wrinkle and a sensibility that feels adjacent to your crime/strangeness overlap.

new.title: The Lazarus Project
new.why: Time-loop espionage, moral pressure, and speculative plotting — very plausible fit.

new.title: Bodies
new.why: Time-spanning conspiracy/mystery structure that fits your taste for long-arc puzzle storytelling.

new.title: Constellation
new.why: A little more cerebral and mood-heavy, but it scratches the identity / reality-slippage itch.

new.title: The Devil’s Hour
new.why: Dark, twisty, and structurally ambitious without feeling like homework.

older.title: Counterpart
older.why: Probably the single cleanest “James show you may have missed”: espionage + parallel-world sci-fi + adult competence.

older.title: Patriot
older.why: Dry, strange, melancholy spy storytelling with a very distinct voice.

older.title: Utopia
older.why: Paranoid conspiracy energy, formal boldness, and a willingness to get strange and ugly.

older.title: Rubicon
older.why: A low-key conspiracy thriller that leans heavily on intelligence work, paranoia, and institutional atmosphere.

older.title: Continuum
older.why: A cleaner old-school sci-fi fit if you want procedural momentum wrapped around time-travel politics.

older.title: Person of Interest
older.why: Starts procedural and then quietly becomes one of the smartest AI / surveillance shows on television.

shared.title: Bad Sisters
shared.why: Darkly funny, well-observed, and ensemble-driven in a way that plausibly overlaps with Hacks / Derry Girls / Mare of Easttown energy.

shared.title: Detectorists
shared.why: Gentle, funny, humane, and specific — a softer shared-watch possibility.

shared.title: Astrid et Raphaëlle
shared.why: French procedural with a strong central duo and exactly the kind of foreign-crime appeal Maggie may share with you.

shared.title: Somebody Somewhere
shared.why: A warmer character-driven recommendation if the shared overlap leans more Bear / Good Place than pure detective work.

highlights.title: 12 unexpected sourdough bread recipes that aren't crusty loaves: From sourdough flatbread to sourdough milk bread — and even sourdough babka.
highlights.notes: Pinned from today's briefing, the kind of bread-nerd rabbit hole worth keeping.

highlights.title: A.I. Is Prompting an Evolution, Not Extinction, for Coders
highlights.notes: Interesting piece on the current state of AI in software work.

highlights.title: The Workers Letting A.I. Do Their Jobs
highlights.notes: In an era of agents powered by artificial intelligence, many programmers are barely programming.

highlights.title: Trump’s presidency is what evil looks like: absurd, frightening, cruel
highlights.notes: Particularly strong Guardian opinion piece, noted for future briefing highlights.
